rulings.Case Title: TERILYN JOE et al VS. 1520 TAYLOR STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION, A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Req For Production Of Docs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, January 7, 2014, Line 1. DEFENDANT 1520 TAYLOR STREET CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. 1. THE OBJECTIONS OF EACH PLAINTIFF AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS.1,11,13-15,25-27 ARE OVERRULED AND EACH PLAINTIFF SHALL SERVE FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES WITHIN 30 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. 2. DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NO. 2 IS DENIED. PLAINTIFFS' RESPONSES ALTHOUGH NOT A RECITATION OF SPECIFIC FACTS SUPPORTING THE ALLEGATIONS, ARE RESPONSIVE TO THE INTERROGATORY AS WORDED. I WOULD ANTICIPATE THAT ANY "FACTS" SUPPORTING THE ALLEGATION OF DETERIORATION WILL BE THE SUBJECT MATTER OF EXPERT TESTIMONY. 3. IT APPEARS THE PLAINTIFFS HAVE SERVED THE REQUESTED VERIFICATIONS AND MADE A PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS RENDERING A NEED FOR AN ORDER ON THAT MATTER MOOT. TO THE EXTENT THAT DEFENDANTS BELIEVE THE PRODUCTION WAS INADEQUATE OR INCOMPLETE THAT SHOULD BE THE SUBJECT MATTER OF A MEET AND CONFER SESSION BETWEEN COUNSEL. 4. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED AS NONE WERE SOUGHT. 5. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS WILL PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, DELIVER TO COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFFS FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND SUBMIT TO THE COURT AT THE HEARING ON JANUARY 7, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NETSANET HAILEMICHAEL VS. BANK OF THE WEST, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday , January 7, 2014, Line 9, DEFENDANT BANK OF THE WEST'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions Granted as to Special Interrogatories nos. 13, 18 granted; denied as to Special Interrogatories nos. 3, 5, 7, 9, 11, 12, 14, 19, 21-31 inclusive as overbroad. (See Obregon v. Sup.Ct. (1998) 67 Cal.App.4th 424, 431.) Granted, in part, as to Requests for Production (RFP) nos. 36, 38, 42; denied as to RFP nos. 35, 37, 39, 40, 41. RFP No. 36, granted in part and limited to the period beginning two years prior to Plaintiff's departure and to the date of production, and the term DEFENDANT is limited to Bank of the West. RFP No. 38 granted to the extent that plaintiff agreed to produce documents within the specified category but did not, and has not confirmed by a verified writing that no such documents exist. RFP No. 42 granted, except the request does not seek emails sent by Plaintiff, unless it is contained in a thread following an email that does fall within this category. Granted as to Form Interrogatories - Employment Law nos. 200.4, 207.2, 212.4(d); denied as to Form Interrogatories - Employment Law nos. 202.1, 202.2, 203.1, 210.4, 212.3, 212.5, 212.7. Plaintiff shall serve verifications for all previous responses to the discovery at issue in this motion and further verified responses, without objection, no later than January 13, 2014. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the SPECIFIC portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: TINA GALLEGOS et al VS. GOLDEN GATE BRIDGE HIGHWAY AND TRANSPORTATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses And Verification To Discovery, And For Attorney Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, January 7, 2014, Line 3. PLAINTIFFS TINA GALLEGOS, GEORGE GALLEGOS'S Motion To Compel Further Responses And Verification To Discovery, And For Attorney Fees And Costs. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. 1 DEFENDANT WILL WITHIN FIFTEEN (15) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER FILE FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION NOS. 1,2,3,&4. DEFENDANT'S RESPONSES FAIL TO COMPLY WITH C.C.P SEC. 2033.220, PARTICULARLY SECTIONS 2033.220 (3)&(C). IF DEFENDANT FAILS TO COMPLY WITH THIS ORDER, THE MATTERS IN EACH SUCH REQUEST FOR ADMISSION WILL BE DEEMED TO BE ADMITTED. 2 DEFENDANT'S RESPONSES TO THE FORM INTERROGATORIES APPEAR TO BE RESPONSIVE WITH THE EXCEPTION OF FORM INTERROGATORIES NOS. 12.2,12.3&12.6 TO WHICH A PRIVILEGE OBJECTIONS WERE MADE. ALTHOUGH DEFENDANTS SERVICE OF THE VERIFICATIONS TO THE VARIOUS DISCOVERY REQUEST WAS UNTIMELY, THE COURT WILL NOT OVERRULE THE OBJECTIONS MADE TO THESE FORM INTERROGATORIES ON THAT BASIS AS COUNSEL APPEARS TO HAVE MADE A GOOD FAITH EFFORT TO OBTAIN SUCH VERIFICATIONS IN A TIMELY MANNER. 3 DEFENDANT WILL DELIVER TO PLAINTIFFS WITHIN THIRTY (30) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER FURTHER RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTION, TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS 3,7,8,9,11,12,13,14,15,19,21,22,23,25&26. 4 PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO THEIR REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS DENIED. DEFENDANT'S ASSERTION OF AN ALLEGED PRIVILEGE AS TO SOME OF THE REQUESTED DOCUMENTS IS NOT OVERRULED BY REASON OF ITS UNTIMELY VERIFICATION; HOWEVER, THIS IS NOT A DETERMINATION AS TO THE VALIDITY OF THE CLAIMED PRIVILEGE. 5 MONETARY SANCTIONS IS THE AMOUNT OF $2,500.00 WILL BE IMPOSED AGAINST DEFENDANT AND ITS COUNSEL AND WILL BE PAID TO PLAINTIFF AND THEIR COUNSEL WITHIN TEN (10) DAY FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. 6 PLAINTIFFS' COUNSEL SHOULD PREPARE AND APPROPRIATE ORDER, DELIVER TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT FOR AGREEMENT AS TO FORM AND SUBMIT THE ORDER TO THE COURT AT THE HEARING ON JANUARY 7, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: GREGORY MCNULTY VS. MOCANA CORPORATION, A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday , January 7, 2014, Line 17, CROSS DEFENDANT GREGORY MCNULTY's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT Overruled. As to the first cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty, the Cross-Complaint adequately pleads all of the elements. (Judicial Council of California Civil Jury Instruction ("CACI") 4103; Cross-Complaint, 6, 14, 21-23.) As to the second cause of action for intentional misrepresentation, the Cross-Complaint adequately pleads all of the elements. (Thrifty Payless, Inc. v. American at Brand, LLC (2013) 218 Cal.App.4th 1230, 1239; Cross-Complaint, 9, 12, 13, 15-17, 34-36.) As to the third cause of action for negligent misrepresentation, the Cross-Complaint adequately pleads all of the elements. (Thrifty Payless, Inc. v. American at Brand, LLC (2013) 218 Cal.App.4th 1230, 1239; Cross-Complaint, 13, 15-17, 34-36.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION,A VS. CESAR ASCARRUNZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-13-535636 WITH CASE CGC-13-534620
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday , January 7, 2014, Line 18, RESPONDENT CESAR ASCARRUNZ'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-13-535636 WITH CASE CGC-13-534620 Denied. Plaintiff presented his claim to the Division of Labor Standards Enforcement and received an award in his favor on September 23, 2013. (Declaration of Thomas R. Kaufman, Ex. A.) Defendant's appeal of that award is set for de novo trial on January 27, 2014 ("de novo trial"). Plaintiff's putative class action (Case 13-534620 ) was filed on November 21, 2013; no trial date is set. Consolidating the upcoming de novo trial with the putative class action would unnecessarily delay the de novo trial indefinitely. Plaintiff cites, without explanation, Fireside Bank v. Superior Ct. (Gonzalez) (2007) 40 Cal.4th 1069, but doesn't explain how that case, which seeks to prevent the problems attendant with "one-way intervention" (id. at 1080) militates in favor of consolidation here. Therefore, the Court declines to consolidate the two actions pursuant to CCP sec. 1048(a), and, by extension, denies Plaintiff's request to vacate the hearing date for the de novo trial. The Court did not receive a courtesy copy of Plaintiff's late filed reply brief (Local Rule 2.6B), nor is it scanned on the Court's register of actions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm
